This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation."},"item_number":{"type":["number","string"],"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error."},"temporary_id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). 2026-04-01T19:01:14.8625508Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-01T19:00:45.821825719Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57670,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Flaky C4_BgStreamCreate_Cancel tests — cancel races with stream completion\",\"body\":\"## Description\\n\\nThree tests in `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses.Tests.Protocol.CrossApiE2eTests` are flaky — they pass locally and on some CI runs but intermittently fail in the `net - core` pipeline under CI load.\\n\\n### Failing tests\\n\\n1. `C4_BgStreamCreate_Cancel_Then_SseReplay_HasTerminalEvent` — expects `response.failed` but sometimes gets `response.completed`\\n2. `C4_BgStreamCreate_CancelImmediate_ReturnsCancelled` — assertion failure on cancelled status\\n3. `C4_BgStreamCreate_CancelMidStream_ReturnsCancelled` — assertion failure on cancelled status\\n\\n### Root cause\\n\\nAll three tests involve **cancellation racing with stream completion**. Under CI load, the handler completes before cancellation takes effect, so the response ends up `completed` rather than `cancelled`/`failed`. Locally and under lighter load the cancel wins the race and the tests pass.\\n\\n### Evidence\\n\\n- **PR #57478** (introduced the tests, merged 2026-03-31): all 3 tests passed across 3 net8.0 CI jobs\\n- **PR #57668** (unrelated Azure.Identity.Broker release): all 3 tests failed\\n- **Local runs**: all 3 tests pass consistently (6/6 runs)\\n- **No code changes** to `sdk/agentserver/` between PR #57478 and the observed failures\\n\\n### Example CI failure\\n\\n```\\nAssert.That(lastEvent.EventType, Is.EqualTo(\\u0026#34;response.failed\\u0026#34;))\\n  Expected string length 15 but was 18. Strings differ at index 9.\\n  Expected: \\u0026#34;response.failed\\u0026#34;\\n  But was:  \\u0026#34;response.completed\\u0026#34;\\n```\\n\\nBuild log: https://dev.azure.com/azure-sdk/590cfd2a-581c-4dcb-a12e-6568ce786175/_build/results?buildId=6094433\\u0026amp;view=logs\\u0026amp;jobId=2c0ca481-defd-57eb-21ab-25ce43c5dcc7\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57670\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"m-nash\",\"id\":64171366,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/m-nash\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/64171366?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-01T18:57:58Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-01T18:57:58Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-01T19:01:14.8645918Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-01T19:00:46.008758905Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57670,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Flaky C4_BgStreamCreate_Cancel tests — cancel races with stream completion\",\"body\":\"## Description\\n\\nThree tests in `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses.Tests.Protocol.CrossApiE2eTests` are flaky — they pass locally and on some CI runs but intermittently fail in the `net - core` pipeline under CI load.\\n\\n### Failing tests\\n\\n1. `C4_BgStreamCreate_Cancel_Then_SseReplay_HasTerminalEvent` — expects `response.failed` but sometimes gets `response.completed`\\n2. `C4_BgStreamCreate_CancelImmediate_ReturnsCancelled` — assertion failure on cancelled status\\n3. `C4_BgStreamCreate_CancelMidStream_ReturnsCancelled` — assertion failure on cancelled status\\n\\n### Root cause\\n\\nAll three tests involve **cancellation racing with stream completion**. Under CI load, the handler completes before cancellation takes effect, so the response ends up `completed` rather than `cancelled`/`failed`. Locally and under lighter load the cancel wins the race and the tests pass.\\n\\n### Evidence\\n\\n- **PR #57478** (introduced the tests, merged 2026-03-31): all 3 tests passed across 3 net8.0 CI jobs\\n- **PR #57668** (unrelated Azure.Identity.Broker release): all 3 tests failed\\n- **Local runs**: all 3 tests pass consistently (6/6 runs)\\n- **No code changes** to `sdk/agentserver/` between PR #57478 and the observed failures\\n\\n### Example CI failure\\n\\n```\\nAssert.That(lastEvent.EventType, Is.EqualTo(\\u0026#34;response.failed\\u0026#34;))\\n  Expected string length 15 but was 18.
